items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 has been cancelled, pushing back consideration of a project at 333 Franklin Street. The hearing will be rescheduled for April 22, 2026, with a new notice to be issued for affected residents.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with new consultant
items.summary: The city signed a third amendment to its consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7th. This ongoing infrastructure project aims to improve traffic and safety at the rail crossing on a major commercial corridor.

items.headline: City awards street pavement repair contract for 2026
items.summary: San José awarded a contract on April 7th for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project, which will fund repairs to residential and local streets across the city. This project addresses ongoing street maintenance needs that residents regularly report.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is advancing Phase 2 of its Active Transportation Plan, which affects how the city prioritizes bike lanes, pedestrian paths, and transit connections. The updated scoring criteria will determine which neighborhood improvements get funding and built first.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract, though the agenda does not specify which project. Residents interested in city infrastructure improvements should review the full meeting minutes for details on the scope and location.

items.headline: Milpitas updates housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 funding strategy, which guides federal housing and development resources. This could affect future affordable housing projects and community development priorities in Milpitas.

items.headline: Major senior housing development approved on East St John
items.summary: A nearly $9M senior apartment complex with 65 units is coming to 678 E St John St, addressing San José's growing need for affordable senior housing. This represents significant new housing stock in a single project.

items.headline: City Council approves emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: The council adopted a new emergency interim housing siting policy on April 1st, which will affect how and where temporary housing for unhoused residents can be located throughout San José. This policy change could impact neighborhoods citywide and represents a shift in how the city approaches its homelessness crisis.
